evidence.q1: Sections numbered 1 through 18 sequentially; cross-reference in Section 16: "all recovery for Section 13 or Section 14 claims is limited to the amount stated in Section 16" resolves correctly.
evidence.q2: "Customer Data", "Deliverables", "Vendor Methods", "Security Incident" defined in Section 2 and used consistently; "Order Form" defined in Section 1 and used throughout.
evidence.q3: "within thirty days after delivery" (Section 4), "forty-five days" (Section 9), "three years" (Section 10), "180 days" (Section 10), "twelve months" (Section 16); no vague time/quantity phrases.
evidence.q4: Contract uses modern commercial language ("will" for obligations, "workmanlike manner"), no archaic terms like "heretofore" or "witnesseth".
evidence.q5: Sample sentence: "If Customer gives written notice within thirty days after delivery, Vendor will correct nonconforming services at no additional charge." No internal contradiction or grammar break.
evidence.q6: Consistent formal register throughout; obligation clauses use "will" uniformly, no sudden shift in diction or tone.
